title: Story Time: An Adventure in Tibet

questions.question: What does "fiction" mean?
options: Fields where barley, a type of grain, is grown.
A story that is made up and not based on real events.
A small part or section taken from a larger text, like a book.
Covered with small stones and pebbles.
questions.correct_answer: A story that is made up and not based on real events.
questions.explanation: "fiction" means: A story that is made up and not based on real events.

questions.question: What does "extract" mean?
options: Fields where barley, a type of grain, is grown.
A small part or section taken from a larger text, like a book.

Covered with small stones and pebbles.
questions.correct_answer: A small part or section taken from a larger text, like a book.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Stories use descriptive language to ______ vivid pictures in your mind.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Stories use descriptive language to create vivid pictures in your mind."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Setting details help you understand where and when a story takes place.
Stories use descriptive language to create vivid pictures in your mind.
Characters in fiction often have strong feelings and reactions.
An exciting beginning can make readers want to know more.
questions.correct_answer: Characters in fiction often have strong feelings and reactions.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Characters in fiction often have strong feelings and reactions." is one of the key things to remember.
